mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or Rank IC > 0.05 in inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust economic demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 40% of periods where both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive growth, or interaction effect size < 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% monthly decline in unemployment (UNRATE) combined with rising payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates tightening labor markets, increasing corporate margins and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 30% of instances where UNRATE declines > 0.5% and PAYEMS rises > 100k, or interaction effect size < 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing activity, increasing corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 35% of periods with INDPRO growth > 1%, or Rank IC < 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% monthly decline in consumer credit (RSAFS) signals reduced household spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days as corporate revenues contract.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances with RSAFS decline > 10%, or Rank IC > 0.05 in contractionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3% monthly increase in unemployment (UNRATE) combined with declining payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates labor market weakness, reducing consumer spending and driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 40% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3% and PAYEMS falls > 50k, or interaction effect size < 0.12.

mechanism_narrative: A 50-basis-point increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) during an inflationary environment (CPIAUCSL > 2%) signals aggressive monetary tightening, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2% and FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5%, or interaction effect size < 0.08.
